violations.description: Mechanical technician was not wearing mechanical gloves as stipulated on the procedure while working on the actuator resulting in a cut on the hand.
When opening the manhole on the intermittent purge drain tank, water came out. The tank was not fully drained.
There was an HTF spill in the area where welding was to take place. The spill had not been adequately cleaned posing a risk of fire during the hot works.
Second welder had not signed the hot work permit.
DC lube oil pump was deisolated while HYDAC was working on the system. This resulted in the pump automatically started resulting in oil spillage through the open piping.
Isolations keys were kept in an uncontrolled/ unlocked area due to LOB unavailability.
Entry vessel without confined space PTW.
Post permit closure audit revealed housekeeping was inadequate.
Post permit closure audit revealed housekeeping was inadequate.
Post permit closure audit revealed housekeeping is inadequate.

Post permit closure audit revealed housekeeping was inadequate and there are missing name tags on some of the newly replaced valves. This is common across all the drain pits where the valves were replaced.
